trust_assumption: A browser/IDE/SaaS application trusts an installed extension as user-sanctioned code entitled to run with the application's full standing session, DOM, and API authority on every page it loads, for as long as it stays installed.
violation: An attacker-published or hijacked extension persists across restarts and silently exercises the host application's standing session and API surface far beyond what the one-time install consent implied.

trust_assumption: Downstream hosts and defenders trust a network boundary device (router/firewall/VPN gateway) to faithfully enforce the segmentation, encryption strength, and system-image integrity its configuration declares, treating the device as a fixed trustworthy control rather than a mutable relay an attacker can re-author.
violation: An attacker who controls the boundary device rewrites routing/NAT to bridge isolated segments, weakens or disables its encryption, or swaps its system image, all while the device continues to report a hardened posture.
